frontmatter.title: Introduction to Cloud Optimized Data on GDEX

affiliations.name: NSF National Center for Atmospheric Research

children.value: GDEX provides Analysis-Ready Cloud-Optimized (ARCO) versions of select datasets in Zarr or virtualized Zarr (kerchunk, virtualizarr) format. ARCO data is chunked and indexed for fast/parallel access — ideal for large-scale analysis like machine learning or AI workflow.
Check whether a dataset has ARCO data available
Obtain the URL link to the ARCO data
Dataset IDs and GDEX web UI
To find ARCO data on GDEX, use the dedicated search filter:
button under the search bar to display all available Analysis-Ready Cloud-Optimized datasets
button for the all the available zarr format dataset.

children.alt: GDEX ARCO filter button under the search bar

children.value: button to show ARCO datasets available in Zarr or virtualized Zarr formats
Once you open a dataset page, use the
tab at the top to view available data links. In the column view, scroll to the desired row (or use the all-data link).

children.alt: GDEX Zarr data access landing page

children.value: Select the data group you want to explore.
The selected link opens a subgroup view of the dataset. Choose the desired subgroup (for example, a specific time frequency).
Select the subgroup you want to use.
You will then reach the final data file page, where you can click the
button to get the Zarr store access URL (example shown: CESM2 Large Ensemble in Zarr format).
button to copy the access link.

%%time
import xarray as xr
# paste the copied URL here
URL = 'https://osdf-director.osg-htc.org/ncar-gdex/d010092/atm/monthly/cesm2LE-ssp370-cmip6-TS.zarr'
xr.open_dataset(URL, engine='zarr')

children.value: ARCO/Zarr data on GDEX enables fast, chunk-aware remote access without full file downloads. Check availability with the API before assuming ARCO exists for a given dataset.
for a complete analysis workflow using ARCO data.
